title: Design System: IMS Managed Services Platform

display.purpose: Hero headline only, one per page.

subhead.purpose: The 'editorial voice' role — secondary line under a section headline, still set in Archivo.

body.purpose: Running copy, capped near a 75ch measure.

label.purpose: The 'technical voice' role — eyebrows, nav labels, mono-styled meta text. Always uppercase, always small.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Default resting elevation for light-surface cards. Large blur, very low opacity, no hard edge.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: More present version of ambient for surfaces needing clearer separation (stat panels, proof cards).

shadows.name: sheen
shadows.purpose: One-pixel inset top highlight simulating a single light source from above on light circular/panel elements.

shadows.name: signal-glow-teal
shadows.purpose: Concentric pulse glow reserved for live/monitored motifs — the operating-loop diagram, status dots. Never generic decoration.

shadows.name: signal-glow-gold
shadows.purpose: Rare gold variant of the signal glow, used on a single status dot.

motion.name: ease-fluid
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover/state transitions across the page.

motion.name: ease-out-strong
motion.purpose: Button lift and primary interactive transforms.

motion.name: ease-drawer
motion.purpose: Hero photo settle-in animation on load.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Overshoot curve reserved for a small, closed set of micro hover-nudges (button icon, loop-node dot, bento arrow). Do not extend to new components — see DESIGN.md Don'ts.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The page's single conversion action. Always resolves to #contact.
components.html: Talk to us→
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; min-height: 52px; border-radius: 999px; padding: 15px 22px; background: var(--navy); color: var(--cream); font-weight: 850; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 14px 36px rgba(7, 27, 52, .22); transition: transform .15s var(--ease-out-strong), box-shadow .2s var(--ease-out-strong); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 18px 46px rgba(7, 27, 52, .30); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(-1px) scale(.97); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--teal-2); outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary__icon { display: inline-grid; place-items: center; width: 30px; height: 30px; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .14); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary, exploratory action — never the primary conversion path.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 52px; border-radius: 999px; padding: 15px 22px; border: 1px solid var(--line-light); color: var(--navy); background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .36); text-decoration: none; font-weight: 850; transition: transform .15s var(--ease-out-strong); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--teal); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Fixed pill-link nav with a brand mark and a persistent CTA that survives every breakpoint.
components.html:
IMS
ProblemShift
Contact Us
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 16px; padding: 14px 22px; } .ds-nav__brand { font-weight: 800; color: var(--navy); text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav__links { display: flex; gap: 4px; } .ds-nav__links a { padding: 9px 11px; border-radius: 999px; color: var(--ink); text-decoration: none; font-size: 14px; transition: color .2s var(--ease-fluid), background .2s var(--ease-fluid); } .ds-nav__links a:hover, .ds-nav__links a.is-active { color: var(--navy); background: rgba(10, 110, 168, .12); } .ds-nav__cta { border-radius: 999px; background: var(--navy); color: var(--cream); padding: 10px 14px; text-decoration: none; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 800; box-shadow: 0 8px 22px rgba(7, 27, 52, .20); } .ds-nav__cta:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--teal-2); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card, Light
components.description: Structural panel on light/cream sections. Sharp 3px radius, ambient shadow, tinted hairline border.
components.html:
Outcome

Mean time to resolution drops when issues are caught before the meeting starts.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--paper); color: var(--ink); border-radius: 3px; border: 1px solid var(--line-light); padding: 28px; box-shadow: 0 24px 80px rgba(7, 27, 52, .12); transition: box-shadow .2s var(--ease-fluid); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 30px 70px rgba(7, 27, 52, .16); } .ds-card__label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal); } .ds-card__title { margin-top: 14px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.4; }

components.name: Card, Navy
components.description: Structural panel on dark sections. Same sharp radius, deeper card shadow, on-navy type color.
components.html:
Pillar

Monitor, detect, resolve — before it becomes a ticket.
components.css: .ds-card-navy { background: linear-gradient(165deg, var(--navy), var(--navy-2) 78%); color: var(--cream); border-radius: 3px; padding: 28px; box-shadow: 0 18px 50px rgba(7, 27, 52, .24); } .ds-card-navy__label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal-2); } .ds-card-navy__title { margin-top: 14px; font-weight: 800; line-height: 1.3; }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: The system's 'technical voice' — small, wide-tracked, uppercase, always Signal Teal.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal); }

components.name: Live Signal
components.description: The system's one signature device — a pulsing status dot used only where something is genuinely live or monitored.
components.css: .ds-signal { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal); } .ds-signal__dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--teal-2); box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(113, 201, 242, .20), 0 0 16px rgba(10, 110, 168, .50); animation: ds-pulse 2.2s var(--ease-fluid) infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: .55; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-signal__dot { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview:
PRODUCT.md states the core positioning in three words: "Operator, not vendor." This system takes that literally rather than figuratively. The page is built like a piece of operational equipment, not a brochure: a faint schematic grid sits under every navy section, structural panels close to square (2–3px radius) rather than softly rounded, a single typeface carries every register from hero display down to technical micro-labels, and the one recurring decorative motif — concentric pulse rings — is a literal monitoring signal, not an abstract flourish.

The voice is vigilant rather than enthusiastic. Color is committed, not restrained-by-default — navy carries most of the page, Signal Teal carries everyday interactivity and live-state feedback, and Precision Gold is spent on almost nothing, which is exactly why it reads as valuable when it appears. This system explicitly rejects generic AI-generated visuals, gradient-and-glassmorphism-as-default, fake metrics or unsupported claims, bloated dependencies, and trend-chasing decoration that will date the page in a year.
keyCharacteristics: A single typeface (Archivo) plays every role — display, body, and mono-style technical label — through weight and tracking alone.
Color strategy is Committed: Deep Navy is the dominant surface and text color across the page, not a 10%-restrained accent.
Precision Gold is the rarest color on the page by design — spent on single moments of proof, never on chrome.
Depth comes from soft, large-blur ambient shadows and a one-pixel top highlight, not hard drop shadows.
The only repeating decorative device is the signal/pulse ring — and it always means "this is live," never "this is decoration."
Shape is tiered by function: sharp for structure, full pill for action, soft tile for icons, true circle for status.

rules.name: The Rare Gold Rule
rules.body: Precision Gold is budgeted, not decorative — it appears at most once or twice per section, always on a number or a status signal, never on UI chrome, links, or body accents.

rules.name: The Tinted Hairline Rule
rules.body: Borders and dividers are never a flat gray. Every line on the page is a low-alpha tint of Ink (on light surfaces) or Instrument Mist (on dark surfaces).

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Every register — display, body, and the mono-styled technical label — renders in Archivo. The system never reaches for a second typeface to manufacture "editorial" contrast.

rules.name: The Negative-Three Floor
rules.body: No heading exceeds -0.03em letter-spacing. Tighter than that and large type starts touching itself instead of reading as designed.

rules.name: The Ambient-Not-Hard Rule
rules.body: Elevation reads through large-blur, low-opacity shadows and a one-pixel top sheen — never a tight, dark, high-opacity drop shadow.

rules.name: The Signal-Glow Exception
rules.body: Concentric ring glows in Signal Teal or Precision Gold mean one thing only: this is being monitored right now. They belong on the pulse diagram and status dots, not on cards, buttons, or unrelated hover states.

dos: Do keep Deep Navy (#002D56) as the dominant surface/text color; it is the system's base register, not an accent to be restrained.
Do spend Precision Gold (#ECAE3D) on at most one or two moments per section — a stat figure, a status glow — per the Rare Gold Rule.
Do render every register (display, body, technical label) in Archivo via weight/size/tracking/case changes, per the One Family Rule.
Do keep structural panels at the 2–3px "drafting precision" radius, full pills (999px) for every interactive control, and true circles for status/signal elements — never mix the three.
Do route the dominant, filled primary button to #contact; reserve the ghost/secondary button for exploratory in-page destinations.
Do use only translucent tints of Ink or Instrument Mist for borders and dividers (Tinted Hairline Rule) — never a flat gray stroke.
Do reserve the concentric Signal-Glow rings for things that are actually live or monitored (Signal-Glow Exception).
Do ship every new animated element with a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce alternative, matching the coverage already present site-wide.
donts: Don't introduce gradients or glassmorphism as a default surface treatment — backdrop-filter: blur() exists on a small, closed set of components; do not extend it to new components.
Don't add a tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section as default scaffolding, or stamp 01/02/03 numbering onto cards that are parallel claims rather than a real sequence.
Don't fabricate metrics, certifications, awards, or client names. Every figure and credential on the page must already exist in approved source copy.
Don't add a second typeface for "editorial" contrast. The One Family Rule exists precisely so nobody reaches for a serif or a mono webfont to signal sophistication.
Don't use a hard, tight, dark drop shadow anywhere. If a shadow needs more presence, reach for the Card shadow, not a harder one.
Don't let the spring/overshoot easing spread beyond the handful of small hover-icon nudges it already drives.
Don't add bloated dependencies or a build step. This is a single static index.html; every new pattern must work as plain CSS/JS dropped into that one file.
Don't chase a design trend that will date the page in a year — this system is built to still look correct in five.
